title: Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT/NPS) Trend Dashboard
industry: SaaS, Retail, Hospitality, Financial Services, Healthcare, Telecommunications — sectors where CX programmes carry recurring revenue at stake and VOC budgets concentrate on XM suites plus transactional surveys.
exclusions: Does not substitute Adobe Launch consent telemetry or storefront checkout abandonment mathematics handled elsewhere in cat-23.1; excludes Session Replay playback licensing for qualitative UX labs; excludes pure mobile-crash instrumentation covered under handset reliability use cases.
evidence: Dashboard Studio workbook VOCUnifiedWeekly archived alongside scheduled PDF snapshots stamped from Splunk alerts routed to CX steering folders.
dataSources:
Unified relational-and-transactional survey telemetry lands in `index=cx_voc_responses` across harmonised sourcetypes produced by REST polling and webhook receivers on Heavy Forwarders or Splunk Cloud HTTP Event Collector.
Primary pulls and pushes:
• Qualtrics XM Platform REST API v3 exports shaped like `/API/v3/surveys/{surveyId}/export-responses` JSON bundles (`qualtrics:survey_export`), plus Qualtrics Webhooks POSTing signed payloads to `/services/collector/event`.
• Medallia Experience Cloud Open Connect API plus Medallia Webhook Listener deliveries normalised as `medallia:signal_webhook` (fields such as signalId, metricId, metricValue, verbatim, collectionDate, unit, customAttributes.case_id).
• SurveyMonkey Enterprise API v3 `/v3/surveys/{id}/responses/{response_id}` rows (`surveymonkey:response`) plus SurveyMonkey Webhooks v2 forwarding completion receipts.
• InMoment XI Platform plus legacy Wootric-style pulls hourly (`inmoment:response`).
• Review ecosystems optional as adjunct VOC streams: Trustpilot Open API stars/text (`trustpilot:review`), Bazaarvoice Conversations API syndicated feedback (`bv:conversation`).
Structured CSV drops scheduled from Forsta (FocusVision Decipher / Confirmit lineage) landed via monitored directories mirror `forsta:survey_export`.
CRM enrichment arrives through Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) against Salesforce Data Cloud snapshots or warehouse mirrors keyed by salted identifiers.
Representative onboarding payloads used during props tuning:
Qualtrics export excerpt — {"responses":[{"responseId":"R_abc","values":{"QID1":9,"QID2":"Excellent service","QID3_1":7},"displayedFields":["QID1","QID2","QID3_1"],"recordedDate":"2024-09-15T14:30:00Z","customer_id_pii_hash":"sha256hex","contact_channel":"email","journey_stage":"post_purchase"}]};
Medallia webhook excerpt — {"signal":{"signalId":"sig_991","signalType":"customer_response","metricId":"NPS","metricValue":9,"verbatim":"billing portal confusing","unit":"enterprise-west","collectionDate":"2024-09-16T03:01:16Z","customAttributes":{"product":"payments_api","support_agent":"jdoe","case_id":"104422"}}};
SurveyMonkey excerpt — {"data":[{"id":"500101","date_modified":"2024-09-16T04:02:31+00:00","custom_value":"acct_h_77","pages":[{"questions":[{"id":"Q1","answers":[{"choice_id":"5_score"}]}]}]}]}.
Index-time parsing (illustrative `props.conf` family for JSON lines): `KV_MODE = json`, `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json`, `SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false`, `LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)`, `TRUNCATE = 0`, `TIMESTAMP_FIELDS = recordedDate,collectionDate,responded_at,date_modified`, `MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 48` with `TIME_FORMAT` variants per vendor; qualify HEC stanzas with `X-Qualtrics-Signature` HMAC-SHA256 verification on the forwarder before indexing.
Role `cx_analytics_role` owns `authorize.conf` `srchIndexesAllowed = cx_voc_responses` for production search heads; hashed fields only in shared indexes.
app: Splunk Add-on for REST ([Splunkbase 1546](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for timed Qualtrics API v3 polling; Splunk HTTP Event Collector plus optional custom Python validators for Qualtrics or Medallia signatures; Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) for Salesforce Data Cloud joins; optional KV Store collections backing `customer_360` and taxonomy version tables when CSV files graduate to collections (`collections.conf`).
spl:
index=cx_voc_responses earliest=-90d@d latest=now
(sourcetype="qualtrics:survey_export" OR sourcetype="medallia:signal_webhook" OR sourcetype="surveymonkey:response" OR sourcetype="inmoment:response" OR sourcetype="trustpilot:review" OR sourcetype="bv:conversation" OR sourcetype="forsta:survey_export")
| eval response_id=coalesce(responseId,id,response_id,signalId)
| eval customer_id_hash=coalesce(customer_id_pii_hash,customer_hash,account_hash,acct_h_77,"")
| lookup customer_360.csv customer_id_hash OUTPUT account_arr_band customer_tier tenure_years support_tickets_last_90d account_health_score benchmark_nps_band
| eval survey_id=lower(trim(coalesce(surveyId,survey_id,"unknown")))
| eval contact_channel=lower(trim(coalesce(contact_channel,"email")))
| eval survey_distribution_pattern=lower(trim(coalesce(survey_distribution_pattern,journey_stage,"transactional_post_event")))
| eval survey_type=upper(trim(coalesce(survey_type,metricId,metric_type,"NPS")))
| eval response_score=tonumber(coalesce(response_score,metricValue,score,star_rating,""))
| eval response_scale_max=tonumber(coalesce(response_scale_max,scale_max,"10"))
| eval verbatim_comment=coalesce(verbatim,open_text,"")
| eval response_lang=lower(trim(coalesce(response_lang,detect_lang,"en")))
| eval responded_at=coalesce(strptime(recordedDate,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),strptime(collectionDate,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),strptime(date_modified,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),_time)
| eval responded_at=if(isnum(responded_at),responded_at,_time)
| eval incident_day=strftime(responded_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup incident_log.csv incident_day OUTPUT major_incident_count
| eval incident_hit=if(major_incident_count>0,1,0)
| eval product=lower(trim(coalesce(product,product_line,"all")))
| eval product_line=lower(trim(coalesce(product_line,business_unit,"")))
| eval region=lower(trim(coalesce(region,geo_region,"")))
| eval segment=lower(trim(coalesce(segment,customer_segment,"")))
| eval csat_topic_tags=coalesce(topic_tag,nlp_primary_topic,null())
| eval survey_platform=case(sourcetype="qualtrics:survey_export","qualtrics",sourcetype="medallia:signal_webhook","medallia",sourcetype="surveymonkey:response","surveymonkey",sourcetype="inmoment:response","inmoment",sourcetype="trustpilot:review","trustpilot",sourcetype="bv:conversation","bazaarvoice",sourcetype="forsta:survey_export","forsta",1=1,"unknown")
| eval normalized_score=round(100*response_score/nullif(response_scale_max,0),1)
| eval nps_category=case(survey_type="NPS" AND response_score>=9,"Promoter",survey_type="NPS" AND response_score<=6,"Detractor",survey_type="NPS","Passive",1=1,null())
| eval csat_t2b=if(survey_type="CSAT" AND ((response_scale_max=5 AND response_score>=4) OR (response_scale_max=7 AND response_score>=6)),1,0)
| eval ces_easy=if(survey_type="CES" AND response_score>=5,1,0)
| bin responded_at span=1w
| eval _time=responded_at
| stats count as responses,
        sum(eval(if(nps_category="Promoter",1,0))) as promoters,
        sum(eval(if(nps_category="Detractor",1,0))) as detractors,
        sum(eval(if(nps_category="Passive",1,0))) as passives,
        sum(csat_t2b) as csat_satisfied,
        sum(eval(if(survey_type="CSAT",1,0))) as csat_responses,
        sum(ces_easy) as ces_easy_count,
        sum(eval(if(survey_type="CES",1,0))) as ces_responses,
        sum(incident_hit) as incident_day_weight,
        sum(eval(if(nps_category="Detractor" AND incident_hit==1,1,0))) as detractor_incident_pairs,
        latest(benchmark_nps_band) as benchmark_nps_band,
        values(account_arr_band) as arr_band_vals,
        values(customer_tier) as tier_vals
    by _time, product, contact_channel
| eval nps_score=round(100*(promoters-detractors)/nullif(promoters+passives+detractors,0),1)
| eval csat_pct=round(100*csat_satisfied/nullif(csat_responses,0),1)
| eval ces_pct=round(100*ces_easy_count/nullif(ces_responses,0),1)
| eval nps_significant=if(responses>=50,"Yes","Insufficient sample")
| lookup product_releases.csv product OUTPUT release_epoch release_type
| eval days_since_release=(_time-tonumber(release_epoch))/86400
| eval is_post_launch_window=if(isnotnull(release_epoch) AND days_since_release>=0 AND days_since_release<=14,1,0)
| eval correlated_to_incident=if(detractor_incident_pairs>0,"Yes","No")
| sort _time
| streamstats current=f window=1 last(nps_score) as previous_week_nps by product, contact_channel
| eval nps_delta=nps_score-previous_week_nps
| eval wilson_margin=if(responses>=100,.03,if(responses>=50,.06,.15))
| eval effect_size=nps_delta*sqrt(responses)
| eval benchmark_floor=tonumber(benchmark_nps_band)-10
| where (nps_significant="Yes" AND abs(nps_delta)>5)
        OR (csat_pct<80 AND csat_responses>100)
        OR correlated_to_incident="Yes"
        OR (is_post_launch_window=1 AND isnotnull(benchmark_floor) AND nps_score<benchmark_floor)
| sort - effect_size
| table _time product contact_channel responses promoters detractors passives nps_score nps_delta previous_week_nps csat_pct ces_pct incident_day_weight detractor_incident_pairs correlated_to_incident is_post_launch_window benchmark_nps_band effect_size wilson_margin arr_band_vals tier_vals
description: Aggregates relational and transactional survey rows from Qualtrics export jobs, Medallia signals, SurveyMonkey responses, InMoment pulls, optional Trustpilot and Bazaarvoice review lines, plus Forsta CSV replay, then coalesces identifiers and question scores into a weekly NPS, CSAT top-two-box, and CES ease-rate series per product and contact channel. The flow normalises disparate score scales to a zero-to-one-hundred common view, applies Net Promoter System class bands, enforces minimum response counts before treating week-over-week deltas as meaningful, joins CDP-derived account tiers, correlates detractor-heavy weeks against major incident calendars, aligns post-release windows to product launches, and layers structured topic tags (from XM-Discover, Lexalytics, or model-assisted tagging) so Splunk mirrors the VOC story your chief customer officer already sees inside native XM consoles—not page-speed Web Vitals (UC-23.1.3), not journey-credit spreadsheets (UC-23.1.5), not single-session form telemetry (UC-23.1.8), and not ecommerce cart abandonment economics (UC-23.1.2).
value: Chief customer officers and VPs of product read the same Splunk-backed week that customer success leads use to prioritise saves, letting portfolio teams re-sequence roadmap funding when transactional CSAT dips after billing changes while relational NPS holds, and letting brand chiefs quantify whether post-service survey channels diverge materially from email-based relationship studies. Finance partners connect account health and ARR tier to score distributions without waiting on quarterly XM Institute style readouts; marketing retains a defensible pulse on competitor-relevant moves when you cross-check Splunk deltas against publicly published industry medians from ACSI, Forrester CX Index, or Bain NPS Prism reference cuts. Operations pairs the stream with Salesforce Data Cloud identifiers so named-account detractors spawn accountable follow-ups rather than anonymous noise.
implementation: Stand up Splunk Add-on for REST (Splunkbase 1546) against Qualtrics token-bound endpoints every fifteen minutes with incremental survey IDs; terminate Qualtrics Webhooks and Medallia Open Connect pushes at Heavy Forwarder HTTPS listeners validating vendor signatures before forwarding into `cx_voc_responses`; mirror SurveyMonkey completion hooks through HEC; schedule hourly InMoment pulls; optionally ingest Trustpilot and Bazaarvoice REST extracts into sibling sourcetypes; salt emails plus CRM IDs into hashed keys prior to indexing and restrict indexes via roles; attach nightly CSV lookups or KV mirrors for customer tiers, benchmark bands, releases, incidents, and NLP topic polarity; save the canonical SPL as a scheduled report feeding Dashboard Studio leadership views and Microsoft Teams plus Slack routing in Step 4 of `detailedImplementation`.
visualization: Dashboard Studio layout with weekly `splunk.line` overlays for `nps_score`, `csat_pct`, and `ces_pct` trellised by `contact_channel`; heatmap (`splunk.matrix`) for product versus channel deltas; ranked `splunk.table` of negative `nps_delta` rows carrying `responses` and `nps_significant`; paired bar chart for promoter versus detractor volumes with passive shading; scatter (`splunk.scatter`) plotting verbatim topic clusters sized by detractor counts after tagging; sparklines comparing Splunk aggregates against CSV benchmarks imported from analyst packs.

references.title: Qualtrics Developer — XM REST API overview
Medallia Developer Portal — APIs including Open Connect
American Customer Satisfaction Index — publications hub
Bain & Company — Net Promoter System topic hub
Splunk Docs — Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: Sudden promoter-ratio erosion frequently mirrors cadence collisions rather than faulty CX craft—when invitation waves overlap renewal invoices plus incident apologies inside one fortnight, irritated respondents dominate returns while quietly satisfied households skip replies altogether, depressing headline scores until throttle maps restore spacing. Concurrent taxonomy refreshes inside XM-Discover or Lexalytics pipelines may shift legacy SERVICE_QUALITY mentions into LOGISTICS friction buckets without any behavioural shift on the floor, producing imaginary topic surges Splunk surfaces as crises until NLP version identifiers reconcile cohort labels.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
Heavy Forwarder or Splunk Cloud HTTP Event Collector endpoints sized for fifteen-minute Qualtrics REST bursts plus webhook bursts during promotional sends; outbound HTTPS permitted to Qualtrics API hosts, Medallia tenants, SurveyMonkey tenants, Trustpilot developer endpoints, optional Bazaarvoice Conversations hosts; Splunk DB Connect identity authorised against Salesforce Data Cloud replica or Snowflake mirror refreshed nightly; Secrets Manager vault storing Qualtrics API tokens, SurveyMonkey bearer tokens, Medallia OAuth materials outside repositories; Splunk roles restricting cx_voc_responses index access to cx_analytics_role plus executive-read clones referencing summarised indexes only.

Step 1 — Configure multi-platform onboarding plus hashing guardrails
Path A — Qualtrics XM REST API v3 via Splunk Add-on for REST (Splunkbase 1546): configure modular inputs targeting Qualtrics REST API v3 survey-response exports (`/API/v3/surveys/{surveyId}/export-responses`) appropriate for tenant throughput; poll every fifteen minutes with persisted checkpoint tokens in checkpoint folder; attach Authorization bearer derived from Qualtrics datacenter-specific tokens; normalise nested JSON via INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS so QID fields flatten into predictable aliases (`response_score`, `verbatim_comment`). Path B — Qualtrics Webhooks streaming POST bodies directly to `/services/collector/event`; implement Heavy Forwarder Python shim validating `X-Qualtrics-Signature` HMAC-SHA256 against shared secrets prior to forwarding payload bodies unchanged into sourcetype qualtrics:survey_export for parity testing.
Path C — Medallia Experience Cloud pairs Open Connect API JDBC-style extracts where architects approve versus asynchronous webhook pushes hitting HEC with sourcetype medallia:signal_webhook; map signal.metricValue into numeric fields before Splunk indexing so CES seven-point scales separate cleanly from eleven-point CSAT scales.
Path D — SurveyMonkey Enterprise uses REST API v3 `/v3/surveys/{survey_id}/responses/{response_id}` polling orchestrated via scripted modular inputs hourly where Burst limits demand spacing; SurveyMonkey Webhooks v2 pushes notify Splunk immediately via secondary token.
Path E — InMoment XI hourly pulls unify legacy Wootric microsurvey intervals plus Allegiance Engage batches.
Path F — Review ecosystems Trustpilot Open API or Bazaarvoice Conversations REST optionally augment relational surveys—convert star distributions cautiously before blending into NPS aggregates.
Path G — Forsta exports scheduled via CSV drops onto SFTP with Splunk batch inputs tagging sourcetype forsta:survey_export.
Hash pipeline — derive customer_email_sha256 and customer_id_sha256 inside transforms prior to unrestricted indexes; retain raw identifiers solely inside privileged indexes governed by Legal.
Illustrative props.conf stanza cluster:
[qualtrics:survey_export]
KV_MODE = json
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TIMESTAMP_FIELDS = recordedDate
TIME_PREFIX = "recordedDate"
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ
TRUNCATE = 0
FIELDALIAS-qalias = responses{}.values.QID1 AS response_score_flat
repeat analogous TIMESTAMP_FIELDS inclusion for collectionDate on medallia payloads.
Search-time FIELDALIAS macros unify survey_id, survey_name, survey_type (NPS, CSAT, CES, PSAT, SUS), response_id, customer_id_hash, account_id_hash, contact_channel (email, sms, in_app, web, voice_ivr, post_chat), survey_distribution_pattern (relational_periodic versus transactional_post_event), response_scale_max, verbatim_comment, response_lang, responded_at, event_correlation_id, product, product_line, region, segment, csat_topic_tags fed by XM-Discover, MonkeyLearn-era clustering, or Anthropic Claude classification batches executed upstream.

Step 2 — Implement composite SPL plus lookups
Paste the canonical SPL from the spl field into Search; extend macros voc_normalize_macro encapsulating eval chains so onboarding engineers reuse transforms across dashboards.
Layer lookups exactly as sequenced in-field: customer_360.csv supplies account_arr_band, customer tier stratifications (Strategic, Enterprise, Mid-Market, SMB), tenure_years, support_tickets_last_90d, account_health_score, benchmark_nps_band sourced from FP&A extracts.
product_releases.csv exposes release_epoch and release_type so fourteen-day post-launch windows isolate roadmap fallout.
incident_log.csv enumerates major_incident_count keyed by incident_day formatted yyyy-mm-dd from Splunk `_time` slices.
nps_verbatim_topics.csv binds csat_topic_tags to topic_polarity_score spanning minus one through plus one based on NLP scoring executed upstream.
Operational correlation macros optionally union Salesforce Service Cloud case extracts once DB Connect pulls Case IDs nightly.
KV Store alternative mirrors CSV rows when simultaneous editors demand versioning.
Collect nightly summaries via summary indexing after validating cardinality caps.

Step 3 — Validation cadence versus vendor consoles plus analysts
Weekly reconcile Splunk weekly promoter minus detractor stacks versus Qualtrics XM-Vocalize tiles filtered identical survey IDs plus invitation cohorts.
Cross-check Medallia CX Reporter dashboards versus medallia sourcetype aggregates segmented same hierarchy metadata.
Contrast SurveyMonkey CX dashboards versus surveymonkey sourcetype totals adjusting API pagination drift.
Validate Trustpilot Brand Performance downloads versus trustpilot sourcetype slices noting apple-app-store versus Trustpilot incomparable scales remain flagged separately.
Compare Splunk-derived gaps versus Bain Net Promoter Prism industry bands, Forrester CX Index sector medians where licences permit, Qualtrics XM Institute maturity narratives strictly as directional—not forensic—checks.
Document translation pipelines whenever multilingual responses route through automated translation layers because SPL cannot introspect linguistic fidelity.

Step 4 — Operational routing plus executive artefacts
Microsoft Teams adaptive cards escalate chief customer officer plus VP Product recipients whenever weekly `nps_delta` drops exceed five points negative alongside `nps_significant="Yes"` and `responses` surpass fifty.
Salesforce Service Cloud Apex or Flow automations spawn cases for individual detractor verbatims tagged with severity when `account_arr_band` identifies strategic ARR thresholds; Slack channel named cx-watchroom streams high-effect-size rows including ARR badges from joined lookup data.
Quarterly executive XM rollup PDF exports append Splunk screenshots with footnotes referencing benchmark tables and survey version identifiers for board audit packs.
Attach runbook links citing Medallia Action Workflow queues for coordinated follow-up.

Step 5 — False positives and interpretation hazards (distinct operational modes)
1. Survey fatigue from more than five touches monthly collapses response mixes toward extremes though latent satisfaction unchanged until cadence caps restore balanced mixes.
2. Thin strata—fewer than fifty completes inside strategic-tier geography slices—produce unstable promoter ratios mistaken for crises.
3. In-app microsurvey prompts triggered after favourable workflows overweight promoters versus sober relationship-email cohorts unless channels remain partitioned.
4. Same respondent scoring promoter on hardware surveys yet detractor on cloud billing surveys hides cross-product variance without dimensional splits.
5. Automated translations distort nuanced Mandarin complaints evaluated via English sentiment dictionaries until native tagging pipelines operate separately.
6. Conservative scoring cultures suppress nine-through-ten ratings versus expressive regions unless Bain-style regional calibration overlays complement raw arithmetic.
7. Major incidents suppress relational scores one-to-seven days afterward yet rebound afterwards—automations must annotate outage overlays instead of blaming roadmap execution alone.
8. Rewritten Likert wording invalidates longitudinal slopes unless survey_version metadata fuels dual-series visuals.
9. Checkout-sent CSAT prompts timed before courier scans diverge materially from prompts timed after proof-of-delivery because respondent populations shift—not necessarily merchandise flaws.
10. CRM merges rewriting historic identifiers temporarily mis-attribute detractors across subsidiaries until Salesforce survivorship completes.
11. Silent abstainers dominate populations—sixty-plus percent delete invitations—while detractors rush replies so headline ratios skew pessimistic absent paired response-rate telemetry.
12. Quarter-end incentive pushes coax favourable ratings that evaporate following payout cliffs unless FP&A overlays incentive calendars.
13. Gift-card incentives lure hurried completions shorter than thirty seconds degrading textual cues Splunk NLP consumes.
14. Aggregating Trustpilot stars alongside XM-derived eleven-point scales yields naive composites unless conversions documented mathematically.
15. Quarterly NLP taxonomy splits reroute SERVICE_QUALITY mentions into narrower logistics facets mimicking emerging complaints absent behavioural shifts.
16. Single-question cellphone pushes outperform fifteen-item questionnaires altering respondent personas whenever programmes swap lengths overnight.
17. Evening versus weekday reply timestamps correlate with terse detractor prose mistaken for magnitude spikes absent scheduling segmentation.
18. Respondents blaming outages frequently cite vendor ecosystems unrelated to internal defects until structured causal annotations augment verbatim feeds.
19. Broad corporate Net Promoter prompts omit SKU specificity leaving detractors unexplained until targeted follow-on pulses activate.
grandmaExplanation: We blend star ratings and written remarks folks send after purchases or service chats, watch whether goodwill climbs or slips week by week, and raise the alarm before renewals turn hostile—not before counting clicks or carts alone.
